calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jun-07-2021 Text Ruling

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar May-03-2021 Continued To Jun-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Continued To May-03-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Etan Rosen for Jeri Rowland.
Konstantine Demiris for and with Trustee, Paul Lucero.
Corinne Rowland.

Mr. Rosen stated the issue is that the trustee has not distributed any money in years, that he was able to talk to Mr. Demiris, that they agree in principle on partial distribution almost immediately, and that they have a system in place toward resolution. The Court asked whether notice and any money from the trust has been given to the homeless beneficiary. Mr. Rosen stated he does not believe the homeless individual, Charles Lucero, Paul's brother, has received his $50,000.00 gift, and has no problem setting those funds aside for the trustee to find his brother. Mr. Demiris stated that his client will be writing distribution checks, that the homeless individual has not been located, that counsels have agreed to have the matter continued to be resolve informally and asked whether the matter should be moved to Contra Costa County. The Court stated everything in this case will be consolidated with the earlier case and the controlling case will be PTR-18-302193. Mr. Rosen stated there is no need to transfer this case to another county if it can be resolved in San Francisco and should be continued for status on the San Francisco property and locating the homeless individual. The petition for instructions is continued to May 3, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Parties to file a joint statement at least 1 week prior.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions
